channel.title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Canada Gives U.S. Arms Makers the Cold Shoulder on Military Spending
item.description: Canada plans to unveil a new strategy that will shift its current reliance on American companies to Canadian military suppliers.

item.title: Europe Today Looks Different From the One Trump’s Team Describes
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio appealed to European leaders in Munich by stressing Christian and cultural bonds that are no longer universal.

item.title: Trump’s border czar says ‘small’ security force will remain in Minnesota after enforcement drawdown. ‘We’ll get back to the original footprint’
item.description: “We already removed well over 1,000 people, and as of Monday, Tuesday, we’ll remove several hundred more,” Homan said.

item.title: U.S. literally can’t afford to lose superpower status as debt looms—so we’re stuck in an ‘increasingly loveless’ marriage with Europe, analyst says
item.description: "Without NATO and its major allies, the U.S. would struggle to maintain its globally dominant role."

item.title: One of Stanford’s original AI gurus says productivity liftoff has begun after doubling in 2025 amid transition to ‘harvest phase’ along J-curve
item.description: "We are transitioning from an era of AI experimentation to one of structural utility."

item.title: Trump Administration Ends Credit for Start-Stop Feature in Cars
item.description: Manufacturers will no longer get a credit toward vehicle emissions standards by installing engines that automatically stop at red lights.

item.title: Winter Storms Could Bring 8 Feet of Snow to Parts of California
item.description: The greatest impact is expected across Northern California, where a pair of powerful storms are expected to begin on Sunday night and last into the we...

item.title: U.S. military tracks down a sanctioned oil tanker from the Caribbean to the Indian Ocean and ‘shut it down’
item.description: “The vessel tried to defy President Trump’s quarantine — hoping to slip away,” the Pentagon said.

item.title: Gasoline-starved California is turning to fuel from the Bahamas
item.description: Last year, California sourced more barrels of gasoline from the Bahamas than it had in the prior nine years combined.

item.title: Like Trump, U.S. Embassies Are Raising Cash for Lavish July Fourth Parties
item.description: Some of Washington’s diplomatic outposts in Asia are raising millions for events to mark the 250th independence anniversary. One ambassador offered to...

item.title: Europe Today Looks Different From the One Trump’s Team Describes
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio appealed to European leaders in Munich by stressing Christian and cultural bonds that are no longer universal.

item.title: Three American Speeches at Munich, and Plenty of Confusion
item.description: As the U.S. message veered from shared heritage and values to shared interests and back again, Europeans wondered what kind of alliance they were left...

item.title: Gisèle Pelicot Speaks
item.description: She was repeatedly drugged by her husband and raped in a series of crimes that shocked the world. Pelicot talked to us about her new memoir.

item.title: U.S. Deports Nine Migrants in Secret, Ignoring Legal Protections
item.description: Most had court orders protecting them from removal to their home countries, so they were sent to detention in Cameroon.

item.title: Canada Gives U.S. Arms Makers the Cold Shoulder on Military Spending
item.description: Canada plans to unveil a new strategy that will shift its current reliance on American companies to Canadian military suppliers.
